title: Can I List a Car Being Sold for Parts? Your UK Guide to Selling Non-Runners
metaDescription: Yes, you can list a car being sold for parts on BuyAndScrap.com. Get expert UK advice on selling your non-runner, from DVLA rules to getting the best price for its components.
targetKeyword: can i list a car being sold for parts
content:
Yes, you can absolutely list a car being sold for parts on BuyAndScrap.com. Many UK car owners have a vehicle that's beyond economical repair. Instead of paying to scrap it, selling for parts can help you recoup some cash. We connect you with buyers specifically looking for these types of vehicles.

## Why Sell Your Car for Parts?

Selling a car for parts is a smart move for several reasons. It turns a problem vehicle into an opportunity.

### When Repairs Are Too Costly
Has your engine blown? Is the gearbox kaput? After an accident, repair bills can easily exceed your car's market value. Avoid a hefty garage bill or paying to scrap it. Selling for parts makes much more sense.

### Giving Life to Other Vehicles
Mechanics and car enthusiasts often need specific components. Your old headlight, a good door, or a working electrical part could be exactly what someone else needs. It helps them get their own car back on the road affordably. You're also doing your bit for the environment through recycling.

## How to List a Car Being Sold for Parts on BuyAndScrap.com

Ready to list a car being sold for parts? Clear and honest communication is vital for a quick sale. Follow these simple steps.

### Be Honest About Condition
Describe your car's overall condition accurately. Highlight any working parts, like a good set of tyres or an undamaged interior. Be equally clear about major faults. This includes engine failure or accident damage. High-quality photos from various angles will help potential buyers immensely.

### Sell the Whole Car, Not Bits
On BuyAndScrap.com, you list the *entire vehicle* for buyers to dismantle. We strongly recommend selling the whole car this way. It's usually simpler legally and logistically than trying to sell individual components. Buyers of 'parts cars' often have the means to transport non-running vehicles. They also handle the dismantling themselves. Start to [Sell Your Car](/sell) with us today.

### DVLA & V5C: The Legal Bits
Selling a whole car for parts still means dealing with the DVLA. You *must* notify them. Give the new owner the V5C/2 'new keeper' supplement. Send the V5C/3 'tear-off' section to the DVLA yourself. The new owner then takes responsibility for the vehicle. Make sure they understand their obligations for proper disposal or re-registration. It's crucial for your peace of mind.

## Common Questions About Selling Cars for Parts

### Can I sell a car without an MOT if it's for parts?
Yes, you can. A car destined for parts doesn't need a valid MOT certificate. It won't be driven on public roads after sale. However, always clearly state its MOT status in your listing. You can quickly [check its MOT history](/mot-checker) if needed.

### What value can I expect for a parts car?
The value depends on several factors. These include the make and model, age, and the condition of its working components. Rare or desirable parts usually fetch more. Even a car with a blown engine can be valuable if it has good body panels or interior.

### What if my car has outstanding finance?
You *cannot* legally sell a car for parts or otherwise if it has outstanding finance. You must settle the finance first. Selling a car with finance attached is illegal and can lead to serious consequences. Always check this before listing.

### How quickly can I sell my parts car?
Sales times can vary. Clear, honest listings with good photos tend to sell faster. Demand for specific makes and models also plays a role. Buyers are often looking for quick deals.

Selling your non-runner for parts is a smart way to get real value. So, if you're wondering 'can I list a car being sold for parts?', the answer is a resounding yes on BuyAndScrap.com! Don't let your old car go to waste. You can also [Browse Cars](/cars) to see what similar vehicles are selling for. Ready to get started?
